title: Simple Network Management Protocol
description: Protocol for monitoring and managing network devices like routers, switches, and servers.
fullExplanation: SNMP allows network administrators to monitor and manage network devices remotely. An SNMP Manager (NMS) queries SNMP Agents running on devices. Each managed device exposes a MIB (Management Information Base) — a hierarchical database of variables (OIDs) describing device state. SNMPv1/v2c use community strings (plaintext passwords). SNMPv3 adds authentication (MD5/SHA) and encryption (DES/AES) — always use v3 in production.

operations.description: Manager requests value of a specific OID from agent
Get next OID in MIB tree (used for MIB walking)
Retrieve large blocks of data efficiently (SNMPv2+)
Manager changes a value on the agent (e.g., disable interface)
Agent sends unsolicited alert to manager (e.g., link down)

versions.feature: Original. Community string auth. No encryption. Avoid.
Adds GET-BULK, 64-bit counters. Still plaintext community strings.
Adds authentication (SHA) and encryption (AES). Use this.

keyFacts: MIB (Management Information Base): hierarchical database of OIDs on each device
Community string 'public' (read) and 'private' (write) are default — change them!
SNMPv1/v2c community strings sent in plaintext — easily sniffed
SNMPv3 USM: User-based Security Model with auth + privacy
SNMP Trap: device proactively alerts manager (link down, high CPU, etc.)
Common uses: bandwidth monitoring, CPU/memory alerts, interface status
Tools: Nagios, Zabbix, PRTG, Cacti, LibreNMS all use SNMP
